content.full_text:
City of Santa Cruz
• CITY ATTORNEY •
9.08 CHURCH STREET
SANTA CRUZ, CALIFORNIA 95060
TELEPHONE (408) 429-3553

December 13, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Conflict of Interest Opinion Request
     Santa Cruz City Councilmember

Dear Sir or Madam:

     This letter is to supplement my December 12, 1990, letter with
regard to the captioned matter. Upon Mr. Rittenhouse's review of
that letter, he noted three factual errors which he wished
corrected.

     At page two, first paragraph, I stated that the Santa Cruz
Hardware Building, on a parcel of property owned by RBIC, is
currently vacant. In fact, that building was destroyed in the
earthquake and, accordingly, the subject parcel is currently bare
land and does not contain a vacant building. Secondly, in that
same paragraph, I refer to two other parcels on Pacific Avenue, one
which formerly contained the Shockley's Jewelry Store Building and
the other which formerly contained the Lily Marlane's Restaurant
Building. In fact, this was one parcel which formerly contained
the two buildings. The third and final correction should be made
with reference to the second paragraph on page two. There I state
that, upon the death of Mr. Rittenhouse's aunt, Mr. Rittenhouse's
father would be entitled to receive the trust assets. In fact,
Mr. Rittenhouse's father would be entitled to receive fifty percent
of the trust assets while Mr. Rittenhouse's two cousins, the two
children of Mr. Rittenhouse's deceased uncle who was named as a
remainderman in the trust along with Mr. Rittenhouse's father, are
each entitled to receive twenty-five percent of the trust assets.
The fact remains that, unless Mr. Rittenhouse's father predeceases
him, there is no guarantee that Mr. Rittenhouse will ever receive
any of these trust assets.

     Thank you for your attention to the foregoing.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signed]
                                        JOHN G. BARTSONE
                                        City Attorney

JGB:md
cc:  Mr. Louis Rittenhouse
     Richard C. Wilson, City Manager

City of Santa Cruz
CITY ATTORNEY
333 CHURCH STREET, 33 PM '50
SANTA CRUZ, CALIFORNIA 95060
TELEPHONE (408) 429-3553

December 12, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Conflict of Interest Opinion Request - Santa Cruz City
Councilmember

Dear Sir or Madam:

As you know, on October 17, 1990, the City of Santa Cruz
experienced a devastating earthquake. As a result of that
disaster, thirty two buildings on a five block section of Pacific
Avenue in the City's downtown district were severely damaged or
destroyed. At this time, the City Council was regularly receiving
permit requests from the affected property owners which, if
granted, would allow the property owners to commence
reconstruction.

After the earthquake the City expanded its Redevelopment
Survey Area to include the entire downtown district including
Pacific Avenue. City Redevelopment Department staff estimate that
approximately fifteen percent of the land within the City is now
part of the Redevelopment Survey Area. Total assessed value of
property within the City approximates two billion dollars of which
$750,000,000 (roughly 1/3) is within the Redevelopment Survey Area.
The City Council serves as the City's Redevelopment Agency.

On November 6, 1990, Mr. Louis Rittenhouse was elected to
serve on the Santa Cruz City Council. Mr. Rittenhouse has a
variety of interests in a number of downtown parcels on Pacific
Avenue. Some of these parcels contained buildings which were
destroyed in the earthquake. Other parcels are located within 300
feet of parcels which contained buildings which were destroyed.
Other parcels are between 300 and 2500 feet from parcels which
contained buildings which were destroyed. Finally, other parcels
are over 2500 feet from parcels which contained buildings which
were destroyed.

The purpose of this letter is to describe Mr. Rittenhouse's
ownership interests in the Rittenhouse parcels on Pacific Avenue
and, in light of those various interests, to ask for advice from
the Commission as to the circumstances under which Mr. Rittenhouse
can properly vote on a variety of issues which will come to the
City Council pertaining to downtown reconstruction.

Fair Political Prac. Comm.
12/12/90
Page 2
_______________________________

     Mr. Rittenhouse is interested in various Pacific Avenue
parcels as a result of his relationship with a corporation and as
a result of his interest in two trusts.  Each of these three
interests will be discussed separately.  First Mr. Rittenhouse
owns stock in the Rittenhouse Building Investments Corporation
(RBIC), a closely held family corporation.  Mr. Rittenhouse owns
ten of the corporation's 2000 shares (i.e. 0.5%).  In addition Mr.
Rittenhouse is an employee of RBIC and he derives approximately 50%
of his income (over $10,000.00) from RBIC.  On Pacific Avenue, RBIC
owns the Flatiron Building (currently vacant).  It also owns the
Santa Cruz Hardware Building (currently vacant).  Both of these
buildings are within 300 feet of the parcel which formally
contained the largest building destroyed as a result of the
earthquake, the St. George Hotel.  Owners of the St. George Hotel
parcel are currently in the process of submitting plans to the City
for a replacement project to be built on that parcel.  In addition,
the RBIC owns two other parcels on Pacific Avenue.  One parcel
formerly contained the Shockley's Jewelry Store Building.  The
other formerly contained the Lily Marlane's Restaurant Building.
Both of these buildings were demolished as a result of the
earthquake and currently each of these parcels is bare land.  Both
of these parcels are within 2500 feet of the St. George Hotel
parcel as well as other parcels of property along Pacific Avenue
which had buildings which were destroyed as a result of the
earthquake.  Both parcels will be the subject of development
proposals in the foreseeable future.

     Secondly, Mr. Rittenhouse is the contingent remainderman to a
trust which was established by his grandmother (now deceased).  Mr.
Rittenhouse's father is the trustee.  Mr. Rittenhouse's aunt is the
trust beneficiary who is entitled to receive income from the trust
for her lifetime.  Upon the death of Mr. Rittenhouse's aunt, Mr.
Rittenhouse's father would be entitled to receive the trust assets.
If his father dies before his aunt dies then Mr. Rittenhouse would
divide his father's share of the trust assets with his sister.
Because both his aunt and his father are alive, there is no
guarantee at this time that Mr. Rittenhouse will ever receive any
of the trust's assets.  The trust corpus is comprised of stocks and
bonds and a parcel of property on Pacific Avenue which is now bare
as a result of the earthquake.  That parcel, like the parcel owned
by RBIC, is within 300 feet of some parcels along Pacific Avenue
upon which construction will eventually take place, within 2500
feet of other such parcels, and over 2500 feet from other such
parcels.  The parcel will be the subject of a development proposal
in the foreseeable future.  As trustee, Mr. Rittenhouse's father
has the discretion to retain this parcel of property or to sell it.
Accordingly it is possible that the parcel may not be part of the
trust corpus when and if trust assets are eventually received by
Mr. Rittenhouse.

Fair Political Prac. Comm.
12/12/90
Page 3
_____________________________

     Finally, Mr. Rittenhouse is a remainderman under another
trust.  This trust was established by Mr. Rittenhouse's
grandfather. The trustee is Mr. Rittenhouse's stepmother (who is
also secretary of RBIC).  This trust corpus is comprised of two
assets. It has a single parcel of property on Pacific Avenue which
is now bare as a result of the earthquake and which will be the
subject of a development proposal in the foreseeable future. In
addition it has 50% of the stock (i.e. 1000 shares) of RBIC. The
trustee votes this stock.  Trust income is to be divided equally
among Mr. Rittenhouse's father, aunt and uncle (two deceased).
Upon the death of all three trust beneficiaries, the trust property
is to be divided equally among Mr. Rittenhouse, his sister and two
cousins.  Once again the trustee has the discretion to retain the
Pacific Avenue parcel or to sell it.  Accordingly, as with the
other trust, at this time there is no guarantee that Mr.
Rittenhouse will ever take title to the Pacific Avenue parcel owned
by the trust.

     Given the facts set forth in this letter, on behalf of Mr.
Rittenhouse, we would appreciate the Commission's opinion with
regard to the following:

     1.  Under what circumstances may Mr. Rittenhouse, as a
Councilmember or Redevelopment Agency member, participate in Council
Council decisions relative to the development of parcels on Pacific
Avenue which are not owned by RBIC or either of the two Rittenhouse
trusts?

     2.  Are there any circumstances under which Mr. Rittenhouse,
as a Councilmember or Redevelopment Agency member, may participate
in Council or Agency decisions relative to the development of
parcels on Pacific Avenue owned by RBIC?

     3.  Are there any circumstances under which Mr. Rittenhouse,
as a Councilmember or Redevelopment Agency member, may participate
in Council or Agency decisions relative to the development of
parcels on Pacific Avenue owned by either of the two Rittenhouse
trusts?

     4.  As a result of the earthquake a significant percentage of
the Pacific Avenue infrastructure, surface street and other public
improvements will have to be redesigned and replaced.  These
improvements will affect and service all parcels along the subject
portion of Pacific Avenue.  Under what circumstances may Mr.
Rittenhouse, as a Councilmember or Redevelopment Agency member,
participate in decisions pertaining to the redesign and replacement
of public improvements along Pacific Avenue?

Fair Political Prac. Comm.
12/12/90
Page 4

5.     To what extent, if any, is Mr. Rittenhouse as a
Redevelopment Agency member, precluded from participating in
decisions pertaining to development within the Redevelopment Survey
Area or to the appropriation of Redevelopment Agency Funds?

6.     If the Mayor appointed Mr. Rittenhouse to a three
Councilmember "downtown subcommittee" assigned to focus on the
reconstruction of downtown Santa Cruz, would Mr. Rittenhouse's
participation on the subcommittee be subject to the same
limitations outlined in your responses to questions 1 through 5?

We would appreciate the Commission's prompt response to the
foregoing opinion request inasmuch as the City Council is currently
being presented with issues which call all of the foregoing
questions into play.  Thank you very much for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

John G. Barisone
City Attorney

JGB/law

cc: Mr. Louis Rittenhouse
    Richard C. Wilson, City Manager
